member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.BroadcastServer
member.summary: Manages live broadcast relay for an . Handles OBS upload (PUT/POST) and RTMP publish ingestion, FLV muxing, viewer queue fan-out, byte recording, and serves an HTML player page. Call to wire up the default routes on the underlying server.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.BroadcastServer.ViewerQueueCapacity
member.summary: Maximum number of queued byte-array items per viewer before starts dropping frames. A larger value tolerates slow readers at the cost of memory.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.BroadcastServer.ViewerWriteBufferSize
member.summary: Size (in bytes) of the per-viewer write buffer used to batch small FLV tags into larger HTTP chunks. Defaults to 2 MB. Larger values produce fewer, bigger writes which improves throughput at the expense of latency.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.BroadcastServer.StreamKey
member.summary: Gets or sets the stream key required for RTMP publish connections. Publishers must supply this key to begin streaming. A random 24-character key is generated by default. Set to or empty to allow any key.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.BroadcastServer.GenerateStreamKey(System.Int32)
member.summary: Generates a cryptographically random stream key of the specified length using URL-safe alphanumeric characters (A-Z, a-z, 0-9).

member.summary: Raised when the broadcast server wants to write a log message.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.BroadcastServer.Register
member.summary:
Registers the default streaming routes on the underlying :
GET /stream – HTML player page (mpegts.js)
GET /stream/data – raw FLV relay for the player / VLC
PUT /Upload and POST /Upload – OBS upload ingestion
RTMP wildcard publish route

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.Database.ConcatenatedStream
member.summary: A read-only stream that reads from the first stream until it is exhausted, then seamlessly continues reading from the second stream. Used to prepend buffered leftover bytes (already consumed from the socket by ReceiveData) in front of the live NetworkStream so that the TDS read loop processes them in order without missing any data.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.Database.DataServer
member.summary:
MSSQL Server Emulator implementing TDS (Tabular Data Stream) protocol.
Can run standalone (inherits ) or be registered as a protocol handler on via .
All databases, tables and rows live in memory and are persisted to as JSON. Use to import schema and data from an existing SQL Server instance.

member.summary: Server instance name (e.g., "MSSQLSERVER")

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.Database.DataServer.ServerVersion
member.summary: Server version to report to clients (e.g., "17.00.4025" for SQL Server 2025)

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.Database.DataServer.Username
member.summary: Default username for SQL authentication

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.Database.DataServer.Password
member.summary: Default password for SQL authentication

member.summary: Default database name

member.summary: Database that stores the SQL Admin IP whitelist table. When blank, is used.

member.summary: Table used to persist SQL/Admin IP whitelist entries.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.Database.DataServer.ServerName
member.summary: Server name to report to clients

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.Database.DataServer.AllowWindowsAuth
member.summary: Enable Windows authentication (currently defaults to SQL authentication)

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.Database.DataServer.AllowSqlAuth
member.summary: Enable SQL Server authentication

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.Database.DataServer.EncryptionMode
member.summary:
TDS encryption mode negotiated during Pre-Login.
0x00 – ENCRYPT_OFF: Encrypt the login packet only, then revert to plaintext.
0x01 – ENCRYPT_ON: Encrypt all traffic after the TLS handshake.
0x02 – ENCRYPT_NOT_SUP: No encryption at all (clients requiring encryption will refuse).
0x03 – ENCRYPT_REQ: Server requires full encryption.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.Database.DataServer.Certificate
member.summary: X.509 certificate used for TDS encryption (TLS handshake). When and is not 0x02, a self-signed certificate is generated automatically on first use.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.DataServer.GenerateSelfSignedCertificate(System.String)
member.summary: Generates a self-signed X.509 certificate suitable for TDS encryption. The certificate is valid for 10 years and uses a 2048-bit RSA key.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.Database.DataServer.Users
member.summary: Collection of registered users with their passwords

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.Database.DataServer.EnforceSqlLoginIpAllowList
member.summary: When enabled, SQL logins are accepted only from loopback or addresses listed in .

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.Database.DataServer.AllowLoopbackSqlLogin
member.summary: Allows local SQL admin/bootstrap access even when the SQL login IP allowlist is enforced.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.Database.DataServer.AllowedSqlLoginIpAddresses
member.summary: Remote IP addresses allowed to authenticate to SQL when is enabled. Values are also mirrored into when added through .

member.summary: Indicates whether a mutation has been scheduled but not yet persisted.

member.summary: Enables the database key/value cache optimizer. When enabled, simple equality lookups are cached in memory and hot key metadata is persisted so optimized cache indexes can be warmed on the next load.

member.summary: File path used to persist optimized cache metadata. When , the path is derived from .

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.Database.DataServer.CacheOptimizationMaxKeys
member.summary: Maximum number of hot key/value lookups to keep in the optimized cache metadata and warm on startup.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.Database.DataServer.AutoSaveDebounceMs
member.summary: Interval in milliseconds to debounce auto-save writes. Only the last mutation within this window triggers a write.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.Database.DataServer.EnablePayloadEncryption
member.summary: Set this to to keep persisted files in plain JSON. Default is so data is not readable at a glance.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.DataServer.#ctor(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Creates a in hosted mode (no TCP listener).
The owns the listener; this instance only provides the in-memory database engine, persistence, and import capabilities. No port is bound, no standalone client event hooks are installed.
member.param: Must be . Distinguishes this from the standalone constructor.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.Database.SqlAdminPanel
member.summary:
An HTTP-based SQL administration panel (similar to SQL Server Management Studio) that can be enabled on a . When enabled, it registers HTTP routes under /sql that provide:
A login page authenticated against .
An Object Explorer tree showing databases, tables and columns.
A SQL query editor with syntax highlighting.
A results grid for query output.
Enable via or access the panel instance through .

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.Database.SqlAdminPanel.PagesFolder
member.summary: Directory containing HTML page templates for the SQL Admin Panel. Templates use $VariableName placeholders that are replaced at runtime. Defaults to a html folder next to the running application.

member.summary: Base URL path for all admin panel routes.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.Database.SqlAdminPanel.EventDef
member.summary: Represents a saved event definition. Each event has an array of action nodes serialized as JSON.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.SqlAdminPanel.ResolveTable(SocketJack.Net.Database.DataServer,SocketJack.Net.Database.SqlSession,System.String,System.String,SocketJack.Net.Database.Database@)
member.summary: Resolves a potentially qualified table reference against the DataServer. When names an existing database, the table is looked up there; otherwise the qualifier is treated as a schema (ignored) and the table is looked up in the session's current database. Automatically tries dbo.-prefixed and unprefixed variants so that SELECT * FROM Uploads finds dbo.Uploads and vice-versa.

member.summary: Looks up a table by name, automatically trying dbo.-prefixed and unprefixed variants to handle schema-qualified storage.

member.summary: The negotiated TDS encryption mode for this connection. 0x00 = login-only, 0x01/0x03 = full, 0x02 = none.

member.summary: Bytes read from the NetworkStream by the TLS record feeder that turned out to be plaintext (after ENCRYPT_OFF teardown). The read loop prepends these before reading from the NetworkStream.

member.summary: Creates a new TDS protocol handler backed by the given .

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsProtocolHandler.CanHandle(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Returns when the first bytes look like a TDS Pre-Login packet (type 0x12) or a TDS Login7 packet (type 0x10).

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsProtocolHandler.ProcessTdsBuffer(System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Byte},SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection)
member.summary: Processes buffered bytes, extracting complete TDS packets and handling them.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsProtocolHandler.PerformTlsHandshake(SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection,SocketJack.Net.Database.SqlSession,System.Byte[])
member.summary:
Performs a TLS handshake on the connection's underlying TCP stream.
Per [MS-TDS] 2.2.6.5 the TLS handshake is encapsulated inside TDS Pre-Login (0x12) packets. A sits between the and the raw to handle this framing.
After this method returns, is set and all subsequent TDS packets are sent/received over TLS.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsProtocolHandler.RunTdsReadLoop(SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection,SocketJack.Net.Database.SqlSession,System.Byte[])
member.summary:
Runs a blocking TDS read loop that reads TDS packets from the appropriate stream (encrypted or plaintext ) and dispatches them through .
This method blocks the caller (ultimately ReceiveData) so that the TDS handler has exclusive access to the underlying with no competing reader. When the session ends (ENCRYPT_OFF after login, disconnect, or ENCRYPT_NOT_SUP plaintext session) this method returns.
For ENCRYPT_OFF the wrapper's feed-buffer is used: a background thread reads raw TLS records from and pushes them into so that never touches the NetworkStream directly. After teardown the feeder stops and this loop reads plaintext from NetworkStream.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsProtocolHandler.TlsRecordFeeder(SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection,SocketJack.Net.Database.SqlSession)
member.summary:
Background thread that reads raw TLS records from the and feeds them into the feed buffer. This keeps SslStream from ever touching NetworkStream directly, preventing read-ahead that would steal post-teardown plaintext bytes.
A TLS 1.2 record has a 5-byte header: [ContentType(1) ProtocolVersion(2) Length(2)]. Valid content types are 0x14-0x17. After ENCRYPT_OFF teardown the client sends plaintext TDS; when the feeder reads a non-TLS header it stores those bytes as and exits so can consume them.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsProtocolHandler.ReadBytesSync(System.IO.Stream,System.Int32)
member.summary: Reads exactly bytes from the stream synchronously. Returns if the stream ends prematurely.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsProtocolHandler.ContainsStatement(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Returns if the SQL batch contains the given statement keyword at a line/statement boundary (not inside a string). This is a simple heuristic — it looks for the keyword at the start of any line or after a semicolon.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsProtocolHandler.TryHandleServerInfoQuery(SocketJack.Net.Database.SqlSession,System.String)
member.summary: Detects SSMS's GetServerInformation query (which references SERVERPROPERTY) and returns populated result sets matching the multi-result structure SSMS expects. Returns if the query is not a server-info query.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsProtocolHandler.ProcessRpc(SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection,SocketJack.Net.Database.SqlSession,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Handles TDS RPC requests (packet type 0x03). SSMS uses RPC for sp_executesql, sp_prepexec, and similar calls. The RPC payload format differs from SQL batch — it starts with ALL_HEADERS, then a procedure name or ID, followed by parameters.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsProtocolHandler.TryExtractFirstNvarcharParam(System.Byte[],System.Int32)
member.summary: Attempts to extract the first NVARCHAR/NTEXT parameter value from the RPC parameter data starting at . Returns if extraction fails.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsProtocolHandler.WriteCollationEnvChange(System.IO.BinaryWriter)
member.summary: Writes an ENVCHANGE type 7 (SQL Collation) token. Unlike other ENVCHANGE types, the collation payload uses raw bytes (byte-count + octets) rather than Unicode B_VARCHAR. Emits SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS for both new and old values.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsProtocolHandler.SendMultiResultSet(SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection,System.Collections.Generic.List{SocketJack.Net.Database.QueryResult})
member.summary: Sends multiple result sets in a single TDS message (single EOM packet). For each result set, writes COLMETADATA + ROW(s) followed by DONE (0xFD) with DONE_MORE — except the last which gets DONE (0xFD) without DONE_MORE. Per [MS-TDS], SQL batch results use DONE (0xFD); DONEINPROC (0xFF) is only for stored procedures.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsProtocolHandler.WriteResultSetTokens(System.IO.BinaryWriter,SocketJack.Net.Database.QueryResult)
member.summary: Writes COLMETADATA + ROW tokens for a single result set to a BinaryWriter. Does NOT write a DONE token — the caller is responsible for that.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsProtocolHandler.GetTdsStream(SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection,SocketJack.Net.Database.SqlSession)
M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsProtocolHandler.GetTdsStream(SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection)
member.summary: Returns the appropriate stream for TDS I/O, looking up the session from the DataServer's session dictionary.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsProtocolHandler.RunTdsLoop(SocketJack.Net.Database.DataServer,SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection,SocketJack.Net.Database.SqlSession)
member.summary: Runs the TDS read loop for a standalone connection. Called from . After a TLS handshake (triggered by Pre-Login), the loop automatically switches to reading from the encrypted . For ENCRYPT_OFF (login-only), it reverts to plaintext after login.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsWrapperStream
member.summary:
A stream adapter that wraps TLS handshake bytes inside TDS Pre-Login (0x12) packets during the TDS encryption handshake phase.
TLS ClientHello/ServerHello exchange is encapsulated in TDS packets of type 0x12 (Pre-Login). The sees a plain TLS byte stream while this adapter handles the TDS framing on the wire.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsWrapperStream.Passthrough
member.summary: When , pulls from the internal feed buffer (populated by ) and delegates directly to the inner stream without TDS 0x12 framing.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsWrapperStream.UseFeedBuffer
member.summary: When , passthrough reads use the feed buffer (for MutableTcpServer mode where ReceiveData owns the NetworkStream). When , passthrough reads go directly to the inner stream (for standalone DataServer mode).

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsWrapperStream.Preseed(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Pre-seeds the wrapper with bytes that were already read from the network by the ReceiveData loop. These bytes are consumed by before falling back to the inner stream.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsWrapperStream.Feed(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Pushes raw bytes (TLS records) into the feed buffer so that SslStream.Read can decrypt them. Called from when TLS is active.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsWrapperStream.FeedClose
member.summary: Signals the feed buffer that no more data will arrive, unblocking any pending call.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsWrapperStream.Read(System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary:
Reads a TDS 0x12 packet from the inner stream, strips the 8-byte TDS header, and returns the payload (raw TLS data) to the caller (i.e. ).
Per [MS-TDS] 2.2.6.5, TLS handshake bytes SHOULD be wrapped in TDS 0x12 packets, but some clients (notably Microsoft.Data.SqlClient with ENCRYPT_ON) send raw TLS records without TDS framing. This method auto-detects the framing by peeking at the first byte: if it is 0x12 the data is TDS-wrapped; otherwise (TLS content types 0x14–0x17) it is raw TLS and the wrapper switches to passthrough for the remainder of the handshake.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsWrapperStream.Write(System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Wraps raw TLS data in a TDS 0x12 packet and writes it to the inner stream.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsWrapperStream.ReadPassthrough(System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary:
Passthrough read that forwards raw bytes from the underlying stream (or preseed buffer) to without any framing.
handles TLS record parsing internally. This method simply provides the raw wire bytes, draining the preseed buffer first, then reading from the inner stream.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsWrapperStream.ReadFromFeed(System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Reads decrypted-ready bytes from the feed buffer. Blocks until data is available or the stream is closed via .

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Database.TdsWrapperStream.ReadExact(System.IO.Stream,System.Int32)
member.summary: Reads exactly bytes, draining the pre-seed buffer first then falling back to . Returns if the stream ends prematurely.

member.summary: Delegate for upload-streaming routes. The handler receives an from which it can read incoming data chunks (e.g., OBS video) as they arrive on the connection. The handler runs on a background task and should return when it is done consuming data or when returns .

member.summary: Delegate for RTMP publish routes. The handler receives an that yields media chunks as OBS (or another RTMP encoder) publishes. Each chunk is prefixed with a single byte indicating the RTMP message type (8 = audio, 9 = video, 18 = metadata) followed by the raw payload.

member.name: F:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer._activeUploadConnections
member.summary: Tracks connections that have an active handler running. Subsequent raw data on these connections is forwarded to the corresponding instead of being parsed as HTTP.

member.summary: Clears HTTP host restrictions and restores the default allow-all behavior.

member.name: F:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer._activeRtmpConnections
member.summary: Tracks connections running the RTMP protocol (e.g., OBS publishing via rtmp://). Subsequent data on these connections is fed into the state machine instead of being parsed as HTTP.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.HttpTotalBytesSent
member.summary: Running total of bytes sent across all HTTP responses (including headers). This counter persists across connection lifetimes, unlike per-connection counters which are lost when short-lived HTTP connections close.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.HttpTotalBytesReceived
member.summary: Running total of bytes received across all HTTP requests.

member.name: F:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer._httpBytesSentSnapshot
member.summary: Bytes sent via HTTP responses since the last call to . Use this to compute an HTTP bytes-per-second rate from the WPF metrics timer.

member.name: F:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer._httpBytesReceivedSnapshot
member.summary: Bytes received via HTTP requests since the last call to .

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.SnapshotHttpBytesSent
member.summary: Returns the number of HTTP bytes sent since the last snapshot and resets the delta. Call once per second to get a bytes-per-second rate.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.SnapshotHttpBytesReceived
member.summary: Returns the number of HTTP bytes received since the last snapshot and resets the delta. Call once per second to get a bytes-per-second rate.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.Robots
member.summary: Gets or sets the content served at /robots.txt. Set to to disable the built-in robots.txt route. Defaults to a permissive policy.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.AllowDirectoryListing
member.summary: When , mapped directories that contain no index.html / index.htm will return an auto-generated HTML listing of their contents. Defaults to . A .htaccess file inside the directory can override this per-directory with Options +Indexes or Options -Indexes.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.RequestGate
member.summary: Optional request gate. Return a non-empty message to reject the request before routes, static files, streaming handlers, or upload handlers run.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.EndpointSecurity
member.summary: Shared per-IP bot filter and endpoint abuse monitor for this server.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.CacheControl
member.summary: Gets or sets the Cache-Control header value added to every HTTP response. When (the default), no Cache-Control header is emitted. Example values: "public, max-age=3600", "no-cache", "private, max-age=86400". For static files served via MapDirectory, an ETag and Last-Modified header are also sent, and If-None-Match / If-Modified-Since conditional requests are honoured with 304 Not Modified.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.EnablePort80HttpsRedirect
member.summary: Gets or sets whether a non-SSL server bound to port 80 should redirect requests to HTTPS.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.Map``1(System.String,System.String,SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.RouteHandler{``0})
member.summary: Maps a route whose handler receives a deserialized body of type . The request body is deserialized using the configured serializer before the handler is invoked.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.MapHost(System.String,System.String,System.String,SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.RouteHandler)
member.summary: Maps a route that only matches requests whose Host header matches . Exact host names are supported, and wildcard entries such as *.example.com match subdomains.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.MapHost``1(System.String,System.String,System.String,SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.RouteHandler{``0})
member.summary: Maps a host-specific route whose handler receives a deserialized request body.

member.summary: Removes a previously mapped host-specific route.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.MappedRoutes
member.summary: Gets a snapshot of routes registered through .

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.GetMappedRoutes
member.summary: Returns a point-in-time snapshot of routes registered through .

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.MapStream(System.String,System.String,SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.StreamRouteHandler)
member.summary: Maps a streaming route. The handler receives a it can write to line-by-line. The connection stays open until the handler returns.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.MapUploadStream(System.String,System.String,SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.UploadStreamRouteHandler)
member.summary: Maps an upload-streaming route. When an incoming HTTP request matches, the handler runs on a background task and receives an that yields data chunks as they arrive (e.g., continuous OBS MPEG-TS video over HTTP POST).

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.MapRtmpPublish(System.String,SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.RtmpPublishHandler)
member.summary: Maps an RTMP publish route. When an OBS (or compatible) encoder connects via RTMP and publishes to the given name, the runs on a background task and receives an with media data. Use "*" as a wildcard to match any app name.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.MapDirectory(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Maps a local directory to a URL prefix so that files inside the directory are served when a GET (or HEAD) request matches. For example, MapDirectory("/static", @"C:\wwwroot") serves C:\wwwroot\style.css when the client requests /static/style.css. If is "/", the directory is served at the root.
member.param: The absolute or relative path to the local directory.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.MapDirectory(System.String,System.String,System.Action{SocketJack.Net.HtAccessBuilder})
member.summary: Maps a local directory to a URL prefix and configures a .htaccess file using the fluent API for directory-level security.
member.param: The absolute or relative path to the local directory.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.RemoveDirectoryMapping(System.String)
member.summary: Removes a previously mapped directory route.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.MapFile(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Maps a URL path to a specific local file. When a GET (or HEAD) request matches the , the file at is served with the appropriate MIME type. For example, MapFile("/js/widget.js", @"C:\Pages\widget.js") serves the file when the client requests /js/widget.js.
member.param: The absolute or relative path to the local file.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.RemoveFileMapping(System.String)
member.summary: Removes a previously mapped file route.

member.summary: Maps a URL prefix to a virtual document root resolved by application code. Resolvers can serve SQL-backed, sandboxed, or other non-disk content while reusing SocketJack's HTTP MIME, header, and route ordering behavior.

member.summary: Maps a disk directory as an explicit document root. Disk roots delegate to so the existing path containment and .htaccess protections remain authoritative.

member.summary: Determines whether directory listing is allowed for the given local directory. Checks for a .htaccess file first; if it contains Options +Indexes listing is allowed, Options -Indexes denies it. Falls back to .

member.summary: Returns when the request body appears to be fully available in (as opposed to a streaming upload that arrives over multiple reads).

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.ChunkedThreshold

member.summary: Preferred body write size for large fixed-length responses. Default is 4 KB.

member.summary: Cleans up per-connection HTTP state (buffers, active streams, uploads). Called on client disconnection.

member.summary: Set by advanced handlers that write a complete HTTP response directly to the connection stream and want the normal response writer to stand down.

member.summary: Optional file response streamed directly from disk without buffering the full body in memory.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.HttpResponse.StatusLine
member.summary: Builds the full HTTP status line (e.g. HTTP/1.1 200 OK), always falling back to HTTP/1.1 200 OK when any component is missing or empty.

member.summary: Writes the response to using HTTP/1.1 chunked transfer encoding. Each line of text is sent as its own chunk so the client can render progressively. For binary (non-text) bodies, falls back to fixed-size chunks.

member.summary: Wraps a network stream with HTTP/1.1 chunked transfer encoding. Write lines or raw data; each write is sent as an individual chunk and flushed immediately so the client can render progressively. Call (or let the server call it) to send the terminating chunk.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer
member.summary:
A TCP server that detects the incoming byte format (e.g. HTTP request vs SocketJack-framed JSON) and routes each connection to the appropriate . This allows a single listening port to serve multiple protocols simultaneously.
By default a and an are registered. Additional handlers can be added via ; they are evaluated in registration order, with built-in handlers checked last.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.#ctor(System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new that listens on the given port.

member.summary: Initializes a new with explicit options.

member.summary: Registers a custom protocol handler. Custom handlers are evaluated before the built-in HTTP and SocketJack handlers.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.RemoveProtocol(SocketJack.Net.IProtocolHandler)
member.summary: Removes a previously registered custom protocol handler.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.Http
member.summary: Returns the built-in for configuring HTTP routes, directory mappings, and other HTTP-specific settings.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.SocketJack
member.summary: Returns the built-in for reference. SocketJack-protocol connections are also handled through the normal event and registered callbacks.

member.summary: Gets or sets whether the unified SocketJack Admin Suite is enabled. The suite is served at /Admin and keeps the legacy SQL Admin surface available at /sql for compatibility and authentication.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.AdminSuite
member.summary: Returns the instance, or when is false.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.WebSocket
member.summary: Returns the built-in for configuring WebSocket-specific settings. WebSocket connections share the same serialization and callback pipeline as SocketJack connections but use RFC 6455 framing.

member.summary: Raised when a connection's first data is identified as SocketJack protocol. Use this instead of when you need to send SocketJack-framed objects only to SocketJack clients (not HTTP).

member.name: E:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.WebSocketClientConnected
member.summary: Raised when a WebSocket upgrade handshake completes successfully. Use this to perform initialization for browser-based WebSocket clients.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.SqlAdminPanelEnabled
member.summary:
Gets or sets whether the SQL Admin Panel is enabled. When , an HTTP-based administration interface (similar to SQL Server Management Studio) is served at /sql. The panel authenticates against the collection of the registered , automatically creating one if needed.
Features include an Object Explorer tree, a SQL query editor, and a results grid.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.SqlAdminPanel
member.summary: Returns the instance, or when is .

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.SyncPeer(SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection)
member.summary: Overrides peer synchronization so that only connections confirmed as SocketJack receive broadcast peer updates. Without this filter, SendBroadcast pushes SocketJack-framed bytes into every connection's send queue — including HTTP connections — corrupting their response stream.

member.summary: Routes identifier-based sends to the correct protocol handler. On a standard , this wraps the object in a and sends to the server connection. Here we find the actual client connection and use the appropriate framing.

member.summary: Invoked by after a successful WebSocket handshake to raise the event.

member.summary: Returns the backing the registered , automatically registering a hosted-mode handler if one has not yet been added.

member.param: An open (or openable) to the source MSSQL server.
Override the target database name in the DataServer. When , the source connection's name is used.
Optional list of table names to import. When or empty, all user tables are imported.
When (default), all rows are imported. Set to to import only schema (columns).
Maximum number of rows to import per table. 0 = unlimited.

member.summary: Detects and processes SocketJack length-prefixed framed messages. When receives raw bytes that begin with a 15-byte padded length header, this handler extracts the payload, deserializes it through the SocketJack serializer, and dispatches it through the normal pipeline.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.WebSocketProtocolHandler
member.summary: Detects WebSocket upgrade requests and manages WebSocket (RFC 6455) connections inside a . After completing the HTTP upgrade handshake, incoming WebSocket frames are decoded, deserialized through the SocketJack serializer, and dispatched via . This allows browser-based WebSocket clients to share the same port as SocketJack and HTTP clients.

member.name: F:SocketJack.Net.WebSocketProtocolHandler._states

member.name: F:SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection.SuppressConnectionTest
member.summary: When set to , the connection-test poll loop treats every poll as successful. This prevents false disconnection when an (or similar wrapper) has consumed all bytes from the raw socket into its internal buffer, causing Socket.Available == 0 even though the connection is alive.

member.name: F:SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection.RawTcpMode
member.summary: When set to , all SocketJack send/receive/poll processing is disabled for this connection. The protocol handler (e.g. TDS) takes full ownership of the and performs its own I/O. Byte-count metrics are still updated via and .

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection.Active
member.summary: True if connection sending or receiving.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection.BytesPerSecondSent
member.summary: Bytes per second sent on this connection (rolling average over the last 10 seconds).

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection.BytesPerSecondReceived
member.summary: Bytes per second received on this connection (rolling average over the last 10 seconds).

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection.Identity
member.summary: Remote Peer identifier for peer to peer interactions used to determine the Server's Client GUID.
member.returns: NULL if accessed before the Server identifies the Client. To avoid problems please do not acccess this via OnConnected Event.

member.summary: Updates sent-byte metrics without going through the SocketJack send pipeline. Used by protocol handlers running in .

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection.TrackBytesReceived(System.Int32)
member.summary: Updates received-byte metrics without going through the SocketJack receive pipeline. Used by protocol handlers running in .

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection.AdjustThreadPool(System.Int32)
member.summary: Adjusts minimum threads so worker/IO capacity scales with the number of active connections. Without this, the default pool ramp-up (~1 thread per 500 ms) causes server-side deserialization and callback dispatch to fall behind under heavy bot load.

member.summary: When set, this connection uses the raw-byte receive path regardless of . This is activated automatically when the first bytes on the connection look like an HTTP request rather than a SocketJack length header.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions
member.summary:
Default options for , , , and .
These options are used by default unless overridden here.
Set before creating any instances of TcpClient, TcpServer, UdpClient, or UdpServer.
Example:
DefaultOptions.Logging =

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.Serializer
member.summary:
Serializer for both and .
Default is System.Text.Json.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.CompressionAlgorithm
member.summary:
Compression algorithm for both and .
Default is GZip2.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.Logging
member.summary: Output OnConnected, OnDisconnected, and OnConnectionFailed events to Console. Send and Receive events only logged when LogSendEvents or LogReceiveEvents are set to True.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.LogSendEvents
member.summary: Log sent events to console.

summary.para: Log received events to console.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.LogToConsole
member.summary: Log to Debug Output Window.

summary.para: Turns on or off Peer to Peer functionality.
Required to be set before TcpClient.Connect or TcpServer.StartListening.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.UpdateConsoleTitle
member.summary: Update the title of the console window with traffic statistics.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.ConnectionTimeout
member.summary:
Timespan to attempt to connect to a server.
Default is 3 seconds.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.AutoReconnect
member.summary: When True the client will automatically retry connection to the last used Host / Port.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.Backlog
member.summary: Maximum concurrent pending connections.
member.returns: 100 is default. Lower to reduce processing time.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.BindAddress
member.summary: Local address used by TCP servers when binding their listening socket. Defaults to for the existing listen-on-all behavior.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.MaximumBufferSize
member.summary: Maximum buffer size per connection.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.MaximumSendQueueBytes
member.summary: Maximum queued outbound bytes per connection before the connection is closed to protect the host from slow-client memory growth.
member.remarks: Default is 100MB. Set to 0 for unlimited legacy behavior.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.MaximumDownloadMbps
member.summary: Maximum receiving bandwidth.

remarks.para: Default is 0, which means unlimited. Set a positive value to throttle downloads.
Setting 0 also disables explicit receive chunk throttling.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.DownloadBufferSize
member.summary: Download buffer size. Default is 65536 bytes. Integer

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.MaximumUploadMbps
member.summary:
Maximum Upload bandwidth.
Default is 0, which means unlimited. Set a positive value to throttle uploads.
Integer

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.UploadBufferSize
member.summary: Upload buffer size. Default is 65536 bytes. Integer

summary.para: Use compression for network transfer.
Must be set before connection started.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.UseHttpCompression
member.summary: Enables HTTP response compression when a client advertises support with Accept-Encoding. This is independent of SocketJack object-stream compression and is safe for browser/API HTTP traffic.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.EnablePatternCache
member.summary: Enables repeated-payload pattern caching for SocketJack TCP/UDP object frames and stable-hash caching for generated HTTP route responses.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.PatternCacheMinimumBytes
member.summary: Minimum serialized payload size before a repeated SocketJack value is considered large enough to promote into the per-connection pattern cache.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.PatternCachePromotionThreshold
member.summary: Number of times the same serialized payload must be seen on a connection before the sender stores it remotely and later sends only its key.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.PatternCacheMaximumBytes
member.summary: Maximum bytes held by each per-connection pattern cache. Older cached values are evicted until a new value can fit.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.EndpointSecurity
member.summary: Bot filtering and endpoint abuse controls for HTTP, SocketJack, WebSocket, and filesystem-backed administrative surfaces.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.HttpAccessLogging
member.summary: Writes one HTTP access log entry per completed request.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.HttpAccessLogDirectory
member.summary: Directory for daily HTTP access logs. Default is C:\LmVsProxy\Logs.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.HttpAccessLogDateFormat
member.summary: Date format used for HTTP access log file names, followed by .log.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.HttpCompressionMinimumBytes
member.summary: Minimum uncompressed HTTP response size, in bytes, before compression is considered. Small responses are usually cheaper to send as-is.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.HttpDefaultCorsOrigin
member.summary: Optional default CORS origin for HTTP responses. Null or empty means SocketJack will not add an Access-Control-Allow-Origin header automatically.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.UseTerminatedStreams
member.summary:
Send and receive objects with terminator (default is ).
to parse objects. An exception would be used in the case of HttpServer where the terminator is not used. HttpServer simply reads to the end of the stream.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.UseSsl
member.summary: Use SSL for network transfer.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.Chunking
member.summary:
When enabled, outbound messages are buffered and flushed in large chunks at the interval specified by .
This reduces the number of small writes and syscalls at the cost of added latency.
Default is .

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.ChunkingIntervalMs
member.summary:
The interval in milliseconds between chunked flushes when is enabled.
Default is 100 ms.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.ChunkingAutomaticLatencyScaling
member.summary:
When , the library automatically adjusts based on measured round-trip latency so chunk flushes stay proportional to network delay.
Requires the built-in ping loop to be active (client-side only).
Default is .

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.Fps
member.summary: Gets or sets the frame rate, in frames per second, for processing operations.
member.remarks: Setting this property adjusts the timing interval used for frame updates. The value should be a positive integer to ensure correct timing behavior. 0 or less = disabled.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.Whitelist
member.summary: Types that are allowed to be deserialized.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.Blacklist
member.summary: Types that are not allowed to be deserialized.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.MaxDatagramSize
member.summary:
Maximum datagram payload size in bytes for UDP.
Datagrams exceeding this size will be rejected with an error.
Default is 65,507 bytes (maximum safe UDP payload). Lower to ~1,400 for safe MTU.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.ClientTimeoutSeconds
member.summary:
Timeout in seconds before a UDP server considers a client disconnected due to inactivity.
Default is 30 seconds.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.UdpReceiveBufferSize
member.summary:
Size of the receive buffer used by the UDP socket in bytes.
Default is 65,535 bytes (maximum single UDP datagram size including headers).

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.EnableBroadcast
member.summary:
When enabled, the UDP socket is configured to allow sending broadcast datagrams.
Default is .

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.ClientTimeoutCheckIntervalMs
member.summary:
Interval in milliseconds between client timeout checks on the UDP server.
Default is 5000 ms (5 seconds).

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.Torrent.TorrentClient
member.summary:
A BitTorrent-style client built on SocketJack's and .
Connects to a to discover peers, then downloads/uploads file pieces using a simplified peer wire protocol. Includes support for searching torrents on the tracker.
Can operate in standalone mode (own for peer connections) or be registered on a to share a port with HTTP, WebSocket, and other SocketJack services.

remarks.code: var client = new TorrentClient(metadata, listenPort: 6883, downloadPath: "./downloads"); var results = await client.SearchAsync("linux iso", category: "software");

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.Torrent.TorrentClient.Torrents
member.summary:
Shared in-memory database that persists the state of all torrent transfers.
Uses with enabled so every mutation is flushed to torrents.json on disk.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Torrent.TorrentClient.Register(SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer)
member.summary:
Registers this torrent client on a , allowing it to share a single port with HTTP, WebSocket, and other SocketJack services.
Torrent peers connect using the SocketJack protocol and their messages are routed through the server's existing callback pipeline. This also creates an embedded on the same server, so the MutableTcpServer acts as tracker + peer server simultaneously.

Start the torrent client: listen for peers, announce to tracker, connect to swarm.
In standalone mode, starts the built-in peer server. In registered mode, the must already be listening.

Register a callback that returns discoverable reflection members.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Services.PromptIntellisense.ExtractTrailingPath(System.String)
member.summary: Extract a trailing file path from the input (e.g. "ai load C:\Users" ? "C:\Users"). Returns null if no path-like segment is found.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.Services.ReflectionService
member.summary: Provides reflection-based execution of properties, fields, and methods on a target object. Results are serialized to JSON using SocketJack-compatible settings with safe fallback for non-serializable types. Supports scanning all loaded assemblies and parsing method parameters.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.Services.ReflectionService.AssemblyInfo
member.summary: Describes a loaded assembly with its types.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.Services.ReflectionService.MemberInfo
member.summary: Describes a discoverable member on the target object.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.Services.ReflectionService.ParameterDetail
member.summary: Describes a parameter of a method.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.Services.ReflectionService.ExecutionResult
member.summary: Result of executing a reflection command.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Services.ReflectionService.GetLoadedAssemblies
member.summary: Gets all loaded assemblies with their public types. Includes framework and application assemblies so /reflect can inspect the full runtime.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Services.ReflectionService.CreateSocketJackJsonOptions
member.summary: Creates the SocketJack JSON options used for safe reflection output.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Services.ReflectionService.TryResolveLoadedType(System.String,System.Predicate{System.Type},System.Type@,System.String@)
member.summary: Resolve a type from loaded assemblies and optionally enforce an allowlist.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Services.ReflectionService.CreateInstance(System.Type,System.String[])
member.summary: Creates an instance with string arguments converted to the constructor parameter types.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Services.ReflectionService.ExecuteStatic(System.String,System.String,System.String[],System.Predicate{System.Type})
member.summary: Executes a static property, field, or method after resolving the type from loaded assemblies.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Services.ReflectionService.SerializeResultValue(System.Object,System.Type)
member.summary: Serializes reflection output with SocketJack-compatible options and safe fallback.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Services.ReflectionService.NavigateTo(System.String)
member.summary: Navigate a dot-separated member path and return a ReflectionService for the resolved object. Returns null if any segment is null or not found.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Services.ReflectionService.ReadMember(System.String)
member.summary: Read the current value of a member by name (property or field).

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Services.ReflectionService.DiscoverMembers
member.summary: Discovers all accessible members (properties, fields, methods) on the target. Excludes compiler-generated, WPF infrastructure, and common noise members.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Services.ReflectionService.GetMatchingMembers(System.String)
member.summary: Get members filtered by a prefix for intellisense.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Services.ReflectionService.GetMethodInfo(System.String)
member.summary: Get parameter info for a specific method by name. Returns null if the method is not found.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Services.ReflectionService.ParseParameters(System.String)
member.summary: Parse parameter string from /reflect command. Parameters are in format (param1, param2, ...) where strings are quoted and numerics are not. Example: (123, "hello world", 45.6, "test")

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Services.ReflectionService.Execute(System.String,System.String[])
member.summary: Execute a member by name, optionally with string arguments. Properties/fields return their value; methods are invoked. Supports parsing parameters from method call syntax: method(arg1, arg2, ...)

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Services.ReflectionService.SafeSerialize(System.Object,System.Type)
member.summary: Safely serialize a value to JSON. Skips non-serializable objects gracefully. Uses SocketJack-compatible serializer options.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.Services.ReflectionService.IsFileType(System.Type)
member.summary: Returns true for types that represent file-like inputs (byte[], Stream, FileInfo, etc.).

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.Services.GitService
member.summary: Executes a narrow, Codex-style Git tool surface for LmVsProxy agent mode. This service builds known git commands itself instead of accepting raw shell text.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.Services.TerminalService
member.summary: Executes local terminal commands for LmVsProxy after the caller has passed the GUI-owned permission gate.

member.name: P:SocketJack.ThreadManager.UseGlobalTickLoop
member.summary: When enabled, a single global tick loop will flush send queues for all connections on the same cadence (based on the highest configured FPS across sockets). Disabled by default to preserve legacy per-connection sending behavior.

member.summary: Must be called on application shutdown to ensure all threads are closed.
